3. **Understanding Fault Adjacency**: A fault adjacent mutant would cause changes in the behavior of multiple tests—some may start passing while others fail. In this case, one test was fixed (passing), but several others that were previously passing now failed. This indicates that the change has ripple effects across different parts of the system.

4. **Conclusion**: Since modifying line 359 affects multiple tests differently (both fixing and breaking some), it suggests that the mutant is fault adjacent. The mutation impacts various test cases, showing its influence on different areas of the code.
